key_insight: For shallow polynomial neural networks with affine targets, the loss has zero infimum always, but true minimizers exist only when there are at least as many hidden neurons as the activation degree; for the cubic case, all critical points can be fully classified and non-global minima are highly constrained.
engineering_relevance: This gives a precise landscape map for training polynomial neural nets, helping practitioners anticipate when optimization will diverge, when exact fit is possible, and what critical-point structures to expect.
method: Analyze the true loss for one-dimensional shallow polynomial neural networks with monomial activation and affine target.
Derive existence/non-existence conditions for global minimizers as a function of hidden width and activation degree.
Classify all critical points for cubic activation, including saddles, global minima, and non-global local minima via pivot structure.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical results only: a sharp minimizer criterion for arbitrary degree and a complete critical-point classification for the cubic activation case.
watch_item: The guarantees are specific to shallow 1D polynomial networks and affine targets, so the landscape behavior may not transfer to deeper networks, higher-dimensional inputs, or non-polynomial activations.
paper_title: Landscape analysis for shallow neural networks: Complete classification of critical points for cubic activation and affine target functions
